default: Good question — let me pull this from our scheme catalogue.
I have checked what we track in our **Schemes** catalogue for you.
overview: Here is how our funding catalogue breaks down today.
Let me give you the big picture on grants and subsidies we track.
nl_hub: For Dutch subsidies, the official finder is usually the best first stop.
If you are operating in the Netherlands, start here.
nl_schemes: Looking at Netherlands schemes for your question.
These are the NL programmes that best match what you asked.
uk_schemes: For United Kingdom operators, these schemes are worth a look.
Here is what we have on file for UK funding.
eu_schemes: EU-wide programmes can sit alongside national schemes — here is what matches.
Cross-border funding often stacks with local subsidies; start with these.
restaurant: Restaurant and hospitality funding is a strong part of our catalogue.
For food-service businesses, these schemes mention your sector.
equipment: If you are upgrading kitchen equipment, these schemes often come up first.
Equipment and efficiency upgrades are a common reason operators ask about grants.
deadlines: Worth noting these dates — but always confirm on the official page.
These schemes have a deadline recorded in our catalogue.
finance_portal: Not sure where to browse? These portals are the main Greenways entry points.
I can point you to the schemes portals and finance finder.
product_grants: Product grants are one of the most practical parts of Greenways — here is how they work.
When you shop marketplace products, matched funding travels with the item.
why_grants: Subsidies matter because they lower the risk of choosing efficient equipment.
This is the role funding plays in the sustainability transition.

media: That is moving fast in the news — **Cheryce** tracks those headlines properly.
For fresh policy news, **Cheryce** is the better specialist — I will hand you over.
